articles.summary: Trust is a critical part of any deal. Customers, big and small, need to know how you’ll protect their data before they can do business with you. If you’re not prepared, this can lead to lengthy questionnaires, long security reviews, and awkward sales conversations. Use this five-step checklist for busy executives

articles.title: SQUARE’S OUTAGE
articles.summary: Payments company Square had an outage on Thursday last week. This happens of course, but the impact on businesses that use Square to take payments can be huge. The event added fuel to the ongoing culture war around migrating to a cashless society.

articles.title: CONFIDENCE VS ARROGANCE
articles.summary: As a founder, you have to be confident in what you’re doing. That’s how you sell people on the vision to invest in you, work with you, buy from you. However, you need to understand the boundary between confidence and arrogance.

articles.title: REGULATING ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE
articles.summary: Slowing the pace of innovation allows competitor countries to catch up. Letting innovation run unfettered can unlock a Pandora's Box of problems. This article spins an interesting story that showcases why regulating artificial intelligence is so difficult.

articles.title: INTEGRATION STRATEGY
articles.summary: As you scale, the number of integrations you have with providers is going to increase. Your CRM, Finance stack, ERP, and collaboration tools all need to talk to each other. Interoperability between applications is a continuous strategic decision for founders. You need to have an owner of this strategy in your startup.

articles.title: WHEN TO BORROW DEBT
articles.summary: Selling equity in your startup is the most expensive form of financing. Debt is cheaper, but only accessible under certain circumstances, like when you know your unit economics, have product market fit, and have proven repeatable channels to find customers.

articles.title: THE TOP 10 MISTAKES FOUNDERS MAKE AFTER $10M ARR
articles.summary: From stepping away from founder-led sales completely to hiring mistakes. Here’s a list of some things you can be mindful of as a founder of a scaling startup that’s in the growth phase.

articles.title: IF YOU WANT TO TRICK INVESTORS, TELL THEM YOUR CAAR
articles.summary: Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) is a staple metric of investor expectations. Contracted Annual Recurring Revenue (CAAR) is being used more by founders to show unrealized revenue from committed contracts. It bumps the numbers, but it might leave you in hot water further down the line.

articles.title: HOW AI CAN SPARK BOTH TECHNOLOGICAL AND BUSINESS MODEL INNOVATION
articles.summary: Truly great innovation comes in pairs. Technology innovation and business model innovation. They work together and deliver outlier results. AI is the next technology innovation, but have we found its counterpart on the business model side yet?

articles.title: SECURING AI IS THE NEXT BIG PLATFORM OPPORTUNITY
articles.summary: When it comes to AI and security, there is a significant amount of work and effort required. This article breaks down the risks and challenges and looks at the opportunities for companies in this new world order.

articles.title: WORKING REMOTELY DOESN’T ALWAYS MEAN FROM HOME
articles.summary: Remote can be anywhere that inspires you to work at your best.

articles.title: SAY/DO RATIOS
articles.summary: “Under-promise and over-deliver” - how many times have we heard that? The mantra is focused on ensuring you only take on enough work that you can really do justice. But are you in danger of being seen as a slacker? There’s a balance somewhere.

articles.title: COMPANY CULTURE IS THE LAST 50 DAYS
articles.summary: The incorrigible Jason Fried of 37 Signals gives his take on culture and how to view it around the things that have happened around a 50-day rolling window. Best quote: “Culture is the nonfiction story of an organization. It writes itself.”
